Imagine a wave packet hitting an energy barrier. A small part of the wave bleeds through the wall. This allows particles to tunnel, defying classical physics. Scanning Tunneling Microscopes measure this effect. Electrons tunnel across a tiny gap to map atoms. This phenomenon powers stars and enables modern electronics.
